steps.title: Identify what the equation represents
steps.content: The equation $3x + 5y = 32$ models a total — the 32 cups of broth are split between small and large jars. This means the left side breaks down the distribution: some term represents cups in small jars, and another represents cups in large jars, and they sum to 32.

steps.title: Determine what each coefficient means
steps.content: Since $x$ = number of small jars and $y$ = number of large jars, and the equation adds up cups (not jars), the coefficients 3 and 5 must be the capacity of each jar type. That is: 3 cups per small jar, and 5 cups per large jar. So $3x$ gives total cups in all small jars, and $5y$ gives total cups in all large jars.

steps.title: Interpret 5y
steps.content: \$5y = 5 \times (\text{number of large jars}) = (\text{total cups in the large jars})$. This is the product of jar capacity (5) and jar count ($y$). That matches choice C — C is correct.
